description_long: This is a classic corded telephone with a rectangular base and a separate handset connected by a coiled black cord. The base features a numeric keypad with clearly defined buttons, a small display screen, and several additional function buttons, likely for speed dial or other telephony features. The handset rests securely in a molded cradle on the right side of the base, designed for easy lifting and replacement. The phone is primarily a light beige or off-white color, with the cord and some button details in black. The overall design is utilitarian and robust, typical of office or home phones from the late 20th century. The materials appear to be mostly hard plastic with some internal metal components for structural support and electronic function.
description: A beige corded telephone with a numeric keypad, display screen, and a coiled black cord connecting the handset to the base. The design is functional and typical of office or home desk phones.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full keypad, display, and the handset resting in its cradle with the coiled cord visible.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the sloped profile of the base and the ergonomic shape of the handset.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the base and the cord extending from the handset.
description_view_3: Angled rear-side view showing the handset partially off the cradle and the coiled cord trailing behind.
